quipHello: The light runs on schedule. Ish.
quipProjects: A light is just a promise you keep every night.
quipNotes: Written by a human, edited by deadline.
quip404: You are the first person to find this exact wrong URL. Probably.
heroKicker: Justin Smith · senior software engineer, Pittsburgh PA
heroHeadline: I help keep the lights on behind the news.
heroBody: Backend engineering at the Post-Gazette: a Kubernetes platform designed and built solo, 29 services, a couple million visits a month, a healthy amount of boxes-and-arrows. Dad the rest of the time. Serial picker-upper of hobbies.
dict: 1. the Argo, but for one 2. argc, but forever 3. a sea of water, or of stars 4. what a method signature becomes if I'm not supervised
bottleProverbs: A migration nobody notices is a migration done right.
The queue does not care about your deadline. Respect it anyway.
Every cron job is a small promise to your future self.
The best on-call shift is the one you slept through.
Name it well and you will never have to explain it.
The boat that leaves on schedule was loaded the night before.
Cache invalidation, naming things, and one more thing I forget.
Ship the boring version. Boring floats.
A rollback is not a failure. It is a lifeboat.
The sea provides. Usually a merge conflict.
